title: Changes in the implementation of the additional financial obligation applied on passenger cars
bodyHtml:
Two major changes have been made to the implementation of the additional financial obligation applied on passenger cars.

The first change is that the additional financial obligation applied on passenger cars originating from the People’s Republic of China under the additional customs duty has been removed.

The second change introduces additional financial obligations, in addition to the customs duty, through new footnotes added to the Import Regime Decree, as outlined below:

  1. For hybrid vehicles   capable of being charged by plugging to external source of electric power : An additional financial obligation of 30% or a minimum of USD 7,000 per unit, whichever is higher, will be applied in addition to the customs duty. No additional financial obligation will be applied to imports benefiting from customs duty exemption under an investment incentive certificate.
  2. For passenger cars  with only electric motor for propulsion: An additional financial obligation of 30% or a minimum of USD 8,500 per unit, whichever is higher, will be applied in addition to the customs duty. No additional financial obligation will be applied to imports benefiting from customs duty exemption under an investment incentive certificate.
  3. For other passenger cars: An additional financial obligation of 25% or a minimum of USD 6,000 per unit, whichever is higher, will be applied in addition to the customs duty. No additional financial obligation will be applied to imports benefiting from customs duty exemption under an investment incentive certificate.

The additional financial obligation will only be applied to countries that do not have a free trade agreement with Türkiye, as well as to GSP countries.

According to the provisional article in the Decision introducing these changes, if the customs declaration for goods subject to a newly introduced or increased additional financial obligation is registered within 60 days following the publication date of the Decision (i.e. by 22 November 2025), the additional financial obligation applicable prior to the entry into force of the Decision will continue to be applied.

Decision Amending the Import Regime Decree and the Decision on the Application of Additional Customs Duties on Imports (Decision No: 10436)
